message:
fix: preserve custom WebSocket close codes, persist load-shed state, and tighten HTTP/1 parsing

Three protocol/service correctness fixes and one minor test cleanup:

src/net/websocket/close.rs — preserve custom close codes on the wire:
- Add raw_code field to CloseReason so application-defined codes
  (3000–4999) survive parse→encode round-trips without being discarded
  when they don't map to a known CloseCode enum variant
- Add wire_code() accessor for callers that need the original u16
- Encode/to_frame/is_normal now prefer raw_code over the enum, ensuring
  the peer receives the exact code that was sent
- Update tests to assert raw_code preservation including a new test for
  custom code 3001

src/service/load_shed.rs — fix premature overload flag reset:
- Previously, calling an overloaded service immediately cleared the
  overloaded flag, allowing the next call through even if the inner
  service was still not ready. Now the overloaded flag persists until
  poll_ready observes the inner service as ready again, preventing
  request leakage during sustained back-pressure.
- Add ToggleReadyService test helper and
  load_shed_keeps_shedding_until_ready_again regression test

src/http/h1/codec.rs — extract header name constants and tighten parsing:
- Extract HEADER_TRANSFER_ENCODING / HEADER_CONTENT_LENGTH byte-slice
  constants and is_transfer_encoding_name / is_content_length_name
  inline helpers to reduce repeated string comparisons
- Simplify request_line_end validation: require the hint to be present
  (removing fallback find_crlf path) and bounds-check explicitly

src/trace/canonicalize.rs — minor test improvement:
- Destructure TraceMonoid directly instead of cloning layers in the
  hash-collision spoof test

tests/property_ws_frame.rs — align close code ranges:
- Update arb_sendable_close_code to match the tightened CloseCode
  is_valid_code policy: exclude 1004 (Reserved) from sendable set,
  narrow standard range to 1007–1011, start application range at 3000
- Update close_code_sendability test arrays to include Reserved in the
  non-sendable set and remove it from the sendable set

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
